summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the graph type parameter parsing allows arbitrary characters in the subtype component, which flows directly into include statements, enabling authenticated users to include arbitrary files.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that the graph type regex parsing allows path traversal characters in subtype, which enables local file inclusion.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh verified that html/includes/graphs/graph.inc.php uses regex allowing arbitrary subtype characters, which flows into include path construction without sanitization, enabling LFI.
verification_method: The exploit script analyzes the vulnerable regex and include statement, demonstrating that path traversal characters (../) in the subtype parameter enable arbitrary file inclusion.
evidence_produced_by_run: Confirmed regex parsing at graph.inc.php:8 allows (?P<subtype>.+) matching ANY characters
Demonstrated that ../ sequences in subtype parameter bypass directory restrictions
Verified no basename() or path validation on subtype before include
Proof file created showing LFI vulnerability
limitations_or_blockers: Requires authenticated user to trigger graph rendering
If allow_unauth_graphs=true, this becomes PR:N vulnerability

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the local repository checkout mounted at /target-repo.
